response:
B

The question asks: "A pitcher throws a 0.15 kg baseball at 43 40 m/s towards the catcher. What is the momentum of the baseball while moving at 40 m/s?"

Momentum \( p = m \times v \).

Mass \( m = 0.15 \) kg.

The relevant velocity given in the question for the momentum calculation is explicitly "while moving at 40 m/s", so \( v = 40 \) m/s.

\( p = 0.15 \times 40 = 6 \) kg m/s.

This matches option C exactly.

(The "43" in "43 40 m/s" appears to be a typo and is not used, as the question specifies the speed for which momentum is requested.)

The correct choice is therefore C.
